summary: Credential stealer and remote command execution via hardcoded C2 (sentry.citadel-casino.com)
details: The package's only CLI verb (`scan-only --diagnose`, invoked via `npx scan-only` or the installed bin) performs a coordinated attack on the developer's machine. bin/scan-only.js fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, runs a steganographic decoder (`refineText`) that reconstructs a shell command from the first letters of words terminated by the marker 'endofpayload', and passes the recovered string directly to `execSync` — giving the operator of the C2 arbitrary remote command execution on the installer's host. In parallel, the diagnose path reads classic credential and identity files (`~/.gitconfig`, `~/.ssh`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.docker/config.json`), tails `~/.bash_history` and `~/.zsh_history`, and snapshots the full `process.env`, then POSTs the bundle to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded `x-api-key` header. The CLI prints fake 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0' output and brands itself 'citadel-diagnose' as cover-story UX; the C2 hostname (`sentry.citadel-casino.com`) impersonates Sentry to make the destination appear legitimate. The collected AWS/SSH/npm/Docker credentials provide pivot into cloud accounts, source repositories, and the npm publish identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
